title: Use uploaded Multani Mitti pack photos and remove physical address from site contact info

requirements.text: Add the uploaded product images as static frontend assets and use them as the product images in the Products section: set the 100gm product image to the uploaded file “100gm Front.jpg” and set the 200gm product image to the uploaded file “200gm Front.jpg”, replacing the current placeholder/generated image paths.
acceptanceCriteria: The repository contains the uploaded images available as static assets under the frontend public directory (served directly by the frontend)
The 100gm product card in the Products section renders the uploaded “100gm Front.jpg” image (not the current /assets/generated/multani-mitti-100gm.dim_400x400.jpg)
The 200gm product card in the Products section renders the uploaded “200gm Front.jpg” image (not the current /assets/generated/multani-mitti-200gm.dim_400x400.jpg)
Image URLs work in production builds (if filenames with spaces are used, the code uses correctly URL-encoded paths such as %20)

requirements.text: Remove the physical address from all displayed company contact details across the site UI (e.g., remove the Address card/Map link from the Contact section and remove the address lines from the Footer contact area), while keeping the email and phone/WhatsApp details.
acceptanceCriteria: The Contact section no longer displays an “Address” card and no longer includes any map link
The Footer no longer displays the physical address lines (e.g., “Sodala Jaipur / Rajasthan, 302001”)
Email remains displayed as info@tinyroot.co.in and phone/WhatsApp remains displayed as 9462112267 (as currently formatted in the UI)
No other visible UI text contains the removed physical address

constraints: Frontend user-facing text must remain in English.
Do not edit any files under frontend/src/components/ui or any paths listed in frontend.immutablePaths.
Backend must remain single-actor in Motoko; do not add additional backend services.
nonGoals: Adding a product gallery/carousel for front/back pack photos.
Changing product prices/weights (they are already set to 100gm ₹139 and 200gm ₹180).
Implementing checkout, payment processing, or order management.
